description: As an environmental scientist analyzing the 'airquality' dataset to model Ozone levels, use Multiple Imputation by Chained Equations (MICE) to address missing values in Ozone and Solar.R. Your analysis should include a visualization of the missing data pattern saved to /home/ga/RProjects/output/missing_pattern.png and a diagnostic plot comparing observed vs. imputed values saved to /home/ga/RProjects/output/imputation_diagnostics.png. Compare a naive linear regression (Ozone ~ Solar.R + Wind + Temp) with a pooled model derived from 5 imputations (seed=123) using Rubin's rules. Export a comparison of the coefficients to /home/ga/RProjects/output/model_comparison.csv with columns 'term', 'estimate_naive', 'estimate_pooled', 'std_error_naive', and 'std_error_pooled'. Record the complete workflow in /home/ga/RProjects/imputation_analysis.R.
